title: Invisible Woman
content:
{{CharHub|Invisible Woman|Sue Storm}} {{AltUniverse|[[Earth-828]]|3=Characters}} {{Stub}} {{Character |image = Invisible Woman Infobox.png |real name = Susan Storm{{Ref|FFFS}} |alias = {{Alias |primary = Sue Storm{{Ref|FFFS}} |codenames = Invisible Woman{{Ref|FFFS}} |derivatives = Suz{{Ref|TFFFS}}
Suzie{{Ref|TFFFS}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Female |DOD = [[1960s/Earth-828|December 1964]] (revived by [[Franklin Richards]]) |title = Doctor |affiliation = {{Affiliation|ANSA}}

{{Affiliation|FUFO}} |movie = ''[[The Fantastic Four: First Steps]]''
''[[Avengers: Doomsday]]'' (unreleased)[https://x.com/DiscussingFilm/status/1817378767787975016 DiscussingFilm on X - July 27, 2024]
''[[Avengers: Secret Wars]]'' (unreleased) |web series = ''[[Future Foundation Public Archives/Promotional Campaign|Future Foundation Public Archives]]'' |comic = ''[[Fantastic Four: First Steps]]''
''[[Fantastic Four: First Foes]]'' (unreleased)[https://thedirect.com/article/mcu-fantastic-four-movie-first-foes-prequel-comic Marvel Unveils Prequel to MCU Fantastic Four Movie, Announces 'First Foes' Comic (Exclusive)] |actor = [[Vanessa Kirby]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|I will not sacrifice my [[Franklin Richards|child]] for this [[Earth|world]]. But I will not sacrifice this world for my child. We will face [[Galactus|this]] together. We will fight this together. And we will defeat this together... as a family.|Sue Storm|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Doctor '''Susan "Sue" Storm''' is the wife of [[Reed Richards]], the older sister of [[Johnny Storm]], the leader of the [[Future Foundation]] and a founding member of the [[Fantastic Four]] known as '''Invisible Woman''', having the ability to become invisible and generate powerful fields of invisible force. One day, Storm discovered that she was expecting a child. Soon after announcing her pregnancy, [[Silver Surfer]] arrived and declared that [[Galactus]] would consume the [[Earth-828|Earth]]. Searching for a way to prevent this, the Fantastic Four traced her energy to another planet across the universe, only to witness it be destroyed by Galactus. They were subsequently caught by Silver Surfer and brought before Galactus, who informed Storm that her child possessed the [[Power Cosmic]] and that he sought to make him his successor. Refusing to give up her child, the team managed to escape, although Galactus forced Storm into premature labor, and she gave birth to a son, whom she named [[Franklin Richards]]. Upon returning to Earth, her decision to keep her child caused the general public to turn against the Fantastic Four. However, Storm managed to quell their anger and unrest by introducing them to her child publicly and assuring them that they would make it through this as a family. When their plan to move the Earth was thwarted by Silver Surfer, Storm ultimately agreed to use Franklin as bait to lure Galactus into their [[Teleportation Bridge]]. Unfortunately, Galactus saw through their trap, and captured him. Seeing this, Storm pushed her powers to their limits and forced Galactus into the bridge, sacrificing herself so Reed could save their child. However, Franklin used his powers for the first time to resurrect her. Four years later, Storm encountered [[Doctor Doom]] interacting with her son in the [[Baxter Building]] and prepared to confront him. ==Biography== {{MD|Susan Storm (Earth-828)}} ===Early Life=== Sue Storm was born on Long Island in [[New York]] and grew up there with her younger brother [[Johnny Storm]] with a shared passion for science.[https://www.instagram.com/p/DMJEz2ORjiA Fantastic Four on Instagram - July 15, 2025] Growing up, Sue's [[John F. Storm|grandfather]] taught her that politics were personal for people and that it affected everyone on a personal level. In her later years, she attended college, where she earned a PhD in archaeology.{{Ref|FFFS}} ===Founding the Fantastic Four=== ====Gaining Abilities==== [[File:Cosmic Radiation.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Sue Storm]] gets hit by [[cosmic radiation]]]] Storm went into space alongside [[Reed Richards]], [[Ben Grimm]], and [[Johnny Storm]], on a mission to gather data on [[cosmic radiation]]. However, they were hit by an unexpected storm of the radiation that penetrated their spacecraft's defenses and each gained abilities, with Sue gaining the ability to turn invisible and create forcefields. The team returned to [[Earth]] on their ship, and Sue's bottom half turned invisible as they all struggled against their powers. At Richards' insistence the team isolated themselves inside [[Building Q]] so that they could understand the changes to their bodies and ensure they were not a danger to those around them.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Isolation==== [[File:Fantasti-Car (The Fantastic Four First Steps).jpg|{{AltFloat|s=200}}|Storm and her companions leave [[Building Q]]]] {{Quote|When do we get to leave?"
Ask another million times, Johnny. Maybe then '[[Reed Richards|Dr. Richards]]' here will finally tell us.|[[Johnny Storm]] and Sue Storm|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Following a significant amount of time in quarantine, [[Johnny Storm]] grew impatient and asked when they would be able to leave. Also tired, Sue Storm told her brother to ask [[Reed Richards]], who she referred to as "Dr. Richards," commenting that he was the one holding them. Richards said that they would be able to leave when he decided they were ready, telling Sue not to be so formal with him. Looking at the computer, Sue saw news reports of an [[Revolt of Subterranea|attack]] in [[New York City]] and got distracted. She turned around to find Storm, Richards, and [[Ben Grimm]] fighting, so she surrounded each of their heads with forcefields, depriving them of oxygen until they stopped. Sue then directed the team's attention toward the news report. Richards struggled to decide whether to leave, so Sue brought him tea and checked on him as Richards was worried about his friends. However, Richards decided to leave, so they flew away from [[Building Q]] together in the [[Fantasticar]].{{Ref|FFFS}} ====[[Revolt of Subterranea]]==== [[File:Fantastic Four vs Giganto (6).png|{{AltFloat}}|Storm and the [[Fantastic Four|team]] defeats [[Giganto]]]] {{Quote|That was the moment everything changed for [[Fantastic Four|us]]: It wasn't the mission, it wasn't the accident. It was coming [[New York City|home]].|Sue Storm|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Storm and the rest of the group stepped out of the [[Fantasticar]] upon reaching [[New York City]] and were surrounded by paparazzi. They looked down into a massive hole in the ground, when [[Giganto]] suddenly came out of it, sending rubble flying everywhere. Storm formed a forcefield around several civilians to protect them from the rubble while [[Reed Richards]] and [[Ben Grimm]] jumped into action fighting Giganto. Storm turned to [[Johnny Storm]] and told him to get in the fighting, saying that they needed him. Johnny was hesitant to frighten people with his powers, but Richards and Grimm convinced him too. While the rest of the team fought Giganto, Sue formed a forcefield around his head, suffocating him and causing him to fall over, allowing the team to regroup a Sue fell to her knees. [[File:Mole Man decides to unleash the Revolt of Subterranea.jpg|{{AltFloat|s=200}}|The [[Fantastic Four]] confront [[Harvey Elder]]]] However, the [[Pan Am Building]] suddenly fell, getting taken underground by the [[Moloids|Moloid]] attackers. Sue Storm and the rest of the team went after the building, Storm protecting herself with a forcefield as she descended. They found [[Harvey Elder]] on the ground floor of the building and confronted him, leading Elder to order the Moloids to attack. Storm fought the Moloids with her forcefield, when one jumped at her from behind with a pick. Richards warned Storm, who trapped her attacker in a forcefield. Elder called out for Storm not to harm the Moloid, so Storm said she was simply containing her. Elder surrendered and called off the attack in exchange for the release of the Moloid, who was pregnant. Storm trapped Elder in a forcefield and took him away while he shouted about power going to the people.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Establishing Peace==== [[File:Subterranea and Surface World make peace.jpg|{{AltFloat|s=200}}|Storm brokers peace with [[Subterranea]]]] {{Quote|Nobody organizes people they don't care about, Mr. [[Harvey Elder|Elder]]. So help me figure out how all the people you care about and all the people I care about can get along.|Sue Storm|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Storm visited [[Harvey Elder]] in his holding cell, where Elder said that he learned from Storm's [[John F. Storm|grandfather]]. Storm did not believe that he was her grandfather's student studying particle physics, so Elder clarified that he worked at the campus' physical plant and that he grandfather taught him how to organize and that he made Elder into a leader. Storm commented on Elder having built a city, but Elder corrected her and said that it was a society. Storm recognized that Elder cared for his people and asked how they could work together to help the people they each cared about. Elder told Storm that his people wanted recognition and prosperity. Storm spoke in front of the [[United Nations/Earth-828|United Nations]] alongside Elder and the two worked to gain recognition for [[Subterranea]]. Storm also helped to reconstruct the destruction caused to Subterranea during the [[Revolt of Subterranea|fight]] using her forcefields. The [[Fantastic Four]] had dinner with all of the [[Moloids]] and held a ribbon-cutting ceremony for the [[Baxter Building]], celebrating their new building together.{{Ref|FFFS}} ===Fame=== ====Interviewed==== Storm was interviewed several times following the [[Revolt of Subterranea]] about the events that happened. In one interview, Storm heard a quote from [[Reed Richards]] in which he explained his mindset leaving quarantine, expressing worry for the rest of the [[Fantastic Four]], whom he referred to as his "cohort." Storm corrected Richards and said that he meant to say "family." In an interview with [[Millicent Collins]] for ''[[Make Mine Millie!]]'', Storm said that [[New York City]] felt like a separate planet upon the Fantastic Four's arrival after their quarantine, saying that that was the moment it had all changed for them. In ''[[The Visible Woman: Sue Storm and Subterranea]]'', Storm talked about how frustrated she was after the revolt that it appeared their powers were being used to attack people from new cultures, and she said that she could not accept that.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Teaching Science==== {{Quote|See, young geniuses? OXYGEN.|Sue Storm|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Storm hosted a telecast called ''[[Kids of S.T.O.R.M.]]'' in order to teach science to children. At one point in the telecast, Storm explained that all living things need oxygen to survive. She then demonstrated it for the audience, proving her point.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Diplomatic Career==== {{Quote|But Sue's an astronaut, a diplomat, and the most accomplished person I've ever met.|[[Reed Richards]]|Meet The Invisible Woman}} On top of her time with the [[Fantastic Four]], Storm also worked as a diplomat and activist. She gave a speech which came to be known as ''[[The Future of Labor]]'', saying in it that people tend to fight for what they love and that all politics are personal and affect people deeply.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Expecting a New Member==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ===[[Advent of Galactus]]=== ====Encountering Silver Surfer==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Rewrite}} *'''Cosmic Energy Enhanced Physiology''': While on a mission to space, [[Sue Storm]] was subjected to [[Cosmic Radiation]], which altered her DNA and gave the cells in her body the ability to produce energies with unique properties allowing her to display the following abilities. **'''Electromagnetic Spectrum Manipulation''': The cells in Sue's body allow her to bend visible light, infrared, ultraviolet, and other forms of light from the spectrum around and away from her body. This produces a number of different effects based on her intention. ***'''Invisibility''': Sue can render herself and other objects invisible by bending the light spectrum around and away from her and her targets. She's able to selectively turn parts of her body invisible. While pregnant, she to turned her stomach invisible in order to show [[Mister Fantastic|Reed]] their [[Franklin Richards|baby]] in order to dissuade his concerns about the baby's biology. ****'''Invisibility Extension''': Storm can extend her invisibility to hide objects and other subjects. Storm was able to turn the [[Fantasticar]] invisible while fighting [[Galactus]], as well as turn the [[Excelsior]] invisible while in labor. This ability does not affect every other sense and is vulnerable to advanced technology. Her ability to extend her invisibility thus far requires physical contact with the object Sue wishes to affect. It is unknown if she can render other objects invisible remotely. ***'''Hyperspatial Force-Field Generation''': The cells in Sue's brain emit an energy that allows her to generate a potent force composed of pressurized space. She can project and shape this force for numerous purposes, such as to block incoming attacks, contain a target, or to move and manipulate objects. Sue's force-fields manifest as a undulating energy that bends the light around it, giving the force a prismatic element. This can be used to refract light and function as a magnifying glass, doing so to enhance her [[Human Torch|brother's]] fire attack when fighting against [[Galactus]], temporarily blinding him. ****'''Force-Field Telekinesis''': Storm is able to move and manipulate objects by creating force fields around said objects. She was able to constrict and throw the [[Silver Surfer]] during the team's escape from [[Galactus]]. When amplified by her emotions, Storm's telekinesis was strong enough to throw the [[Galactus]] into the last remaining [[Teleportation Bridge]] and cause physical damage to his armor. ****'''Force-Field Blast''': Storm can release force-field blasts from her hands by focusing on a target. Storm blasted her brother [[Human Torch|Johnny]] while in labor on the [[Excelsior]] and again in the [[Baxter Building]] to get his attention. She can also use these blasts in more potent fashions as when she angrily blasted a television set. ===Abilities=== *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': ''To be added'' {{Quote|This couldn't be how our these tremendous gifts we'd been given were meant to be used: Encountering new [[Moloids|people]], new [[Subterranea|worlds]], new cultures... and hitting them? I refuse to accept that.|Sue Storm|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} *'''Master Businesswoman''': ''To be added'' ==Equipment== *'''[[Fantastic Four Uniforms|Fantastic Four Uniform]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Digi-Calc]]''': ''To be added'' ===Vehicles=== *'''[[Excelsior]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Fantasticar]]''': ''To be added'' ==Facilities== *'''[[Baxter Building]]''': ''To be added'' ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[John F. Storm]] † - Grandfather *[[Franklin Storm]] - Father *[[Mary Storm]] † - Mother *[[Human Torch|Johnny Storm/Human Torch]] - Brother and Teammate *[[Mister Fantastic|Reed Richards/Mister Fantastic]] - Husband and Teammate *[[Franklin Richards]] - Son and Resurrector ===Allies=== *[[Fantastic Four]] **[[Thing|Ben Grimm/Thing]] - Teammate and Close Friend *[[American National Space Agency]] *[[Future Foundation]] - Subordinates **[[Lynne Nichols]] *[[K.E.R.B.I.E.]] † *[[H.E.R.B.I.E.]] *[[Mole Man|Harvey Elder/Mole Man]] - Former Enemy and Friend *[[John F. Kennedy/Earth-828|John F. Kennedy]] *[[Jackie Kennedy]] *''[[Kids of S.T.O.R.M.]]'' *''[[Make Mine Millie!]]'' **[[Millicent Collins]] - Interviewer *''[[The Ted Gilbert Show]]'' **[[Ted Gilbert]] - Interviewer **[[Sally (Makeup Artist)|Sally]] *[[American Broadcasting Company/Earth-828|American Broadcasting Company]] **[[William Russell]] *''[[Post]]'' **[[Austin Thorogold]] *[[New York City Police Department/Earth-828|New York City Police Department]] *[[Fire Department of New York City/Earth-828|Fire Department of New York City]] *[[Donna (Child)|Donna]] *[[Silver Surfer|Shalla-Bal/Silver Surfer]] - Former Enemy {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Giganto]] *[[Mad Thinker|Julius/Mad Thinker]] *[[Diablo|Esteban Corazón de Pablo/Diablo]] *[[Puppet Master|Phillip Masters/Puppet Master]] *[[Wizard|Bentley Wittman/Wizard]] *[[Red Ghost|Ivan Kragoff/Red Ghost]] *[[Super-Apes]] **[[Peotor]] *[[Galactus|Galan/Galactus]] *[[Doctor Doom|Victor von Doom/Doctor Doom]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TFFFS}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|FFFS}} **{{Appearance|TFFFS}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|FFFF}} (unreleased) *{{Appearance|TFFFS}} **{{Appearance|FFPA}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AD}} (unreleased) *{{Appearance|ASW}} (unreleased) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Sue Storm is the co-founder of the [[Fantastic Four]], having gained her superhuman invisibility and force field abilities from enduring cosmic radiation during a space voyage on a [[Excelsior|shuttle]] she experienced with her other three colleagues. She adopted the superhero alias of Invisible Woman. ==Behind the Scenes== *Invisible Woman in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the fourth live-action incarnation of the character, following incarnations portrayed by [[Rebecca Staab]], {{WPS|Jessica Alba}}, and [[Kate Mara]]. **Alba herself advised [[Vanessa Kirby]] to have fun in the role as she had when she played Invisible Woman in the {{WPS|20th Century Fox}} films ''{{WPS|Fantastic Four (2005 film)|Fantastic Four}}'' and ''{{WPS|Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer}}''.[https://collider.com/fantastic-four-jessica-alba-vanessa-kirby-advice/ Jessica Alba Reveals What ’Fantastic Four’s Vanessa Kirby Needs to Know About Marvel Fans] ==References== {{Gallery|Invisible Woman/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[es:Susan Storm]] [[pt-br:Mulher Invisível]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:The Fantastic Four: First Steps Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Doomsday Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Secret Wars Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Blond Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Astronauts]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:American National Space Agency Members]] [[Category:Fantastic Four Members]] [[Category:Future Foundation Members]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]] [[Category:Revived Characters]]
